providerNotifications: Albumin is < 3.0 or total bilirubin is > 1.5 and if this is not known to the prescriber
New side effects from recent injection
New abnormal involuntary movements, excessive sedation, dizziness, agitation, delirium, fever, tachycardia, abnormal blood pressure, or any concerning symptoms

idealSteps: Dose not yet due. Please consult the haloperidol decanoate early administration guidance.
Administer usual haloperidol decanoate injection **if the dose is 200 mg or less**, otherwise
If the dose is greater than 200 mg, consult provider *before* administering.
If symptoms of psychosis have returned or worsened, recommend oral haloperidol supplementation (consult provider for order).
Schedule a check-in with patient in **6–7 days** (near peak dose) to assess for haloperidol-related adverse effects.
providerNotifications: **Pre-injection:** Consult provider only if haloperidol decanoate dose is greater than 200 mg.
**Post-injection:** Notify provider of the situation and request an order for oral supplementation if needed.

idealSteps: Do not administer. Reinitiation is required. **Consult provider BEFORE any injection.**
providerNotifications: **Before any injection:** Consult provider in all cases.

Schedule a check-in with patient in **6–7 days** (near peak dose) to assess for haloperidol-related adverse effects.

**Post-injection:** Notify provider of the situation and request an order for oral supplementation if needed.
